doc: V1.3 Toy 2 — the Chord player. Seven buttons play the diatonic triads I–vii° of the current key: tap-and-hold sustains a chord, release stops it. Each button sends the `chord` pipe (/chord/in) [degree, gate] (a chord-root scale degree + gate); the `chord` op at /triads stacks scale-relative thirds and emits `degree` notes, which the voicer resolves through the tonal context — so held and tapped chords RE-SPELL LIVE when you change the key (send the `key` pipe, /key/in [48..72]). Each of the 12 voices is a hosted `chord-player-voice` sub-patch (saw -> slow-attack ADSR VCA); the voicer sums them and a `brightness` pipe (/brightness/in [0..1]) tones the mix through a master filter. Polyphonic: 12 voices cover four held seventh chords. Presentation lives in surfaces/chord-player.json.

nodes.doc: Tap-to-harmony: the `chord` pipe (/chord/in) [degree, gate] -> stacked-thirds `degree` notes. `size` 3 = triad (default), 4 = seventh. No harmony input — the voicer resolves degrees, so chords re-spell on key change. The surface doc's 7 chord-buttons each send a fixed root degree 0..6 (I–vii°) through the pipe.
The tonal context the voicer resolves degrees against. KEY SELECTOR: the `key` pipe (/key/in [48..72], MIDI tonic) drives `root` to transpose; held & tapped chords re-spell live. Default C major.

Hosts 12 chord-player-voice sub-patches. Degree notes from /triads re-spell through /harmony; the voicer resolves + allocates them across the voices and sums their audio. 12 voices = four held seventh chords.
Master lowpass on the summed mix; cutoff is a Signal input driven by the brightness Good Button.
Master headroom: a constant equal-power trim (~1/sqrt(12)) smoothed into a CV, so a full 12-voice chord stays off the clip ceiling while a typical 3–6 note chord stays loud.

The PUBLIC `brightness` pipe's held value [0,1] (send /brightness/in) smoothed into a CV Signal (an `m2s` is the value->signal converter), then ranged into the master filter cutoff. The pipe rests at 0.5.
